markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' official delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to May 26, 2026—just days from now—has intensified trader focus on the market, as the near-term release date creates a tight window for any major events or announcements to resolve before launch. The long development cycle, marked by multiple postponements from earlier 2025 targets and sustained anticipation following the 2023 trailer, has kept cultural momentum high around the title's open-world innovations and Vice City setting. Upcoming catalysts like the May earnings call from parent company Take-Two Interactive could clarify final marketing pushes or any last-minute shifts, while ongoing industry chatter about streaming metrics and pre-order tracking adds layers to how traders weigh likely outcomes in entertainment headlines, music drops, or global news against the game's imminent arrival.

The November announcement, issued in late 2025 to allow additional polish after earlier shifts from a 2025 window, remains the binding schedule with no subsequent updates indicating acceleration. While a last-minute pivot cannot be entirely ruled out, the six-month buffer and typical console certification processes create significant barriers, keeping the market-implied probability of a June 2026 launch overwhelmingly low.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent developments in Harvey Weinstein’s protracted legal saga, particularly the May 15 mistrial after jurors deadlocked 9-3 in his third New York rape retrial, have solidified trader consensus around No Prison Time at 77.8 percent. The former Hollywood producer, now 74 and battling documented health issues including cancer, continues serving time under his separate 16-year California convictions, which remain under appeal and would run consecutively to any additional New York outcome. Repeated hung juries, prolonged appeals, and sentencing credits have shifted momentum away from substantial new incarceration, while prosecutors weigh whether to pursue a fourth trial on the same charges. Upcoming rulings on retrial pursuit and California appeals stand as the next key catalysts that could reinforce or modestly adjust these market-implied odds.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup race remains tightly bunched among the final four teams because the Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes both enter their respective conference finals with the strongest regular-season records, deepest rosters, and proven playoff experience, while the Vegas Golden Knights and Montreal Canadiens carry legitimate upset potential through hot streaks and favorable matchups. The Avalanche’s top positioning stems from their league-best point total and consistent depth across all lines, whereas the Hurricanes’ near-even implied probability reflects their Eastern Conference dominance and strong goaltending. Recent second-round results have kept momentum balanced, with no team showing decisive superiority heading into the final stages, underscoring why traders view the outcome as highly competitive.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup race remains tightly bunched among the final four teams because the Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes both enter their respective conference finals with the strongest regular-season records, deepest rosters, and proven playoff experience, while the Vegas Golden Knights and Montreal Canadiens carry legitimate upset potential through hot streaks and favorable matchups. The Avalanche’s top positioning stems from their league-best point total and consistent depth across all lines, whereas the Hurricanes’ near-even implied probability reflects their Eastern Conference dominance and strong goaltending. Recent second-round results have kept momentum balanced, with no team showing decisive superiority heading into the final stages, underscoring why traders view the outcome as highly competitive.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities for the Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs reflects their matchup in the Western Conference Finals, where the Spurs' double-overtime victory in Game 1 has narrowed what was previously a wider gap after both teams posted dominant regular seasons exceeding 60 wins. Victor Wembanyama's impact alongside the Spurs' supporting cast has kept the series competitive, while the Thunder's depth and experience as defending champions maintain their slight edge in trader consensus. In the East, the New York Knicks hold a clear lead over the Cleveland Cavaliers in their conference finals series, though the overall title picture remains fluid given the potential for further upsets or adjustments in series outcomes.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities among France, Spain, and England reflects the exceptional depth across the top European sides as the expanded 48-team tournament approaches, with Spain benefiting from sustained strong form and tactical cohesion after their recent European Championship success, while France retains an edge through squad experience and attacking firepower led by Kylian Mbappé. England and Brazil follow closely due to consistent qualification campaigns and historical pedigree in knockout stages, though Argentina's defending champions status and Portugal's star power create further parity. This competitive dynamic stems from balanced group-stage matchups and recent Nations League results that have kept momentum fluid among contenders without any single team establishing clear dominance.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?

This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
